titles.title: Ultrasensitive Detection of Chemokines in Clinical Samples with Graphene-Based Field-Effect Transistors

record.abstract: Graphene field-effect transistors (GFETs) functionalized with carbon nanomembranes enable ultrasensitive detection of chemokines at attomolar concentrations. A two-step bind-and-read protocol with ionic strength modulation achieves signal amplification through charge unscreening.

titles.title: pH Sensors Based on Amino-Terminated Carbon Nanomembrane and Single-Layer Graphene Van der Waals Heterostructures

record.abstract: We demonstrate pH sensing with graphene-CNM van der Waals heterostructures. The Gouy-Chapman-Stern model describes the electrical double layer at the graphene-electrolyte interface with a functional-layer capacitance accounting for the CNM.

titles.title: Importance of the Debye Screening Length on Nanowire Field Effect Transistor Sensors

record.abstract: We demonstrate that the Debye screening length is a critical parameter for nanowire FET biosensors. At physiological ionic strength the Debye length is approximately 0.8 nm, severely limiting the sensor response to charged biomolecules beyond this distance.

titles.title: Going Beyond the Debye Length: Overcoming Charge Screening Limitations in Next-Generation Bioelectronic Sensors

record.abstract: We review strategies to overcome Debye screening in bioelectronic sensors, including desalting protocols, polymer modifications, and frequency-based methods. The two-step protocol achieves signal amplification by separating binding from readout conditions.

titles.title: Highly Sensitive and Label-Free Detection of SARS-CoV-2 Proteins via Surface Plasmon Resonance

record.abstract: We report SPR measurements of SARS-CoV-2 S-protein and N-protein binding to monoclonal antibodies. The dissociation constants are KD = 0.022 nM for S-protein and KD = 0.57 nM for N-protein.
